CAPABILITY STATEMENT — Tavares LLC (sample 8(a) construction GC capability statement, FL DBE / AK Native)

COMPANY PROFILE: Tavares LLC, established 2007, is a Rhode Island-based, 8(a)-certified general contractor providing reliable, quality general construction. Has completed over 200 projects in ten years, every job on time and within budget; strong safety record with EMR of .69 (among the best in the industry). Self-performs carpentry, painting, drywall, dust/noise protection, demolition, and project supervision.

CONTRACT INFO: DUNS 006493690; CAGE CODE 6PY01.

CORE COMPETENCIES: General Building Construction; Commercial and Industrial Construction; Design-Build; Demolition and Site Work; Historical Renovations; Masonry; Metal Buildings; Environmental Remediation; Lead Paint Removal; self-perform demolition and asbestos abatement.

CORPORATE POC: Fernando Tavares, Sole Owner, Providence, RI.

PRIMARY NAICS: 236210 Industrial Building Construction; 236220 Commercial & Institutional Building Construction; 238130 Framing; 238150 Glass/Glazing; 238310 Drywall & Insulation; 238320 Painting/Wall Covering; 238350 Finish Carpentry; 562910 Remediation Services. Bonding capacity: $15,000,000. Licensed General Contractor.

SB CERTIFICATIONS: WOSB, 8(a) (expires 11/25/2025), HUBZone, SDVOSB, SDB.

MAJOR CUSTOMERS / PAST PERFORMANCE: Dept. of the Interior / National Park Service (DOI/NPS); Naval Facilities Engineering Command (NAVFAC); DCAMM; Providence Public Schools; Harvard University; Northeastern University; Massachusetts Port Authority; and primes Gilbane, Suffolk Construction, Dimeo, Whiting-Turner, Skanska, Bond Brothers, Shawmut.

REPRESENTATIVE PROJECTS (with role, prime, value, scope):
- Boott Cotton Mills, Lowell MA (DOI/NPS) — Historic Windows, $2,942,548: replaced ~335 deteriorated wood windows with custom aluminum windows matching 19th-century textile-mill appearance.
- South Street Landing Enabling, Providence RI — Demolition & Asbestos Abatement (Prime: Gilbane), $5,333,602: paint removal on steel/masonry, lead-paint abatement, general demolition, asbestos caulking removal.
- Lowell Justice Center (DCAMM) — Doors/Frames/Hardware (Prime: Dimeo), $1,921,928.
- CambridgeSide Galleria Mall — Drywall & Demolition (Prime: Whiting-Turner), $3,245,653: ~200,000 sf common-area renovation, LED-lit soffits, drywall ceilings.
- Citizens Bank Refresh, New England — General Trades (Prime: Gilbane), $1,522,004: night/weekend branch renovations while open.
- Walgreens, Providence RI — General Trades (Prime: Gilbane), $1,327,000: ground-up construction, all trades.
- RI Veterans Home, Bristol RI — Windows, $2,113,133.
- College of the Holy Cross — Doors/Frames/Hardware & Painting (Prime: Bond Brothers), $1,800,312.
- Longmeadow High School — Asbestos Abatement & Demolition (Prime: Gilbane), $2,610,500.
- NAVFAC Newport Naval Base — Scaffold/Staging at Building 1275, $34,500.
- Easton Building, Boston (DOI/NPS) — Rehabilitation of Floors 1 & 2, $1,191,500: asbestos floor-tile removal, glass/glazing, wheelchair lift, plumbing, fire protection, HVAC, lighting, cabinets.

Teaching point: a strong capability statement leads with certifications + bonding capacity, lists core competencies and the exact NAICS codes the firm self-performs, and proves past performance with named agencies/primes, dollar values, and concrete scope.
